title: Putney for River Air and a Proper Music Pub
subtitle: A low-key Thames evening that can stay as a walk and a pint, or turn into live music if the mood holds.

seo.title: Putney for River Air and a Proper Music Pub
seo.description: A walk-first Putney route with Thames air, an old-pub pause and an optional live-music ending.

quickFacts.endStation: Putney Bridge Underground or Putney rail
quickFacts.noiseLevel: moderate; loud if a gig is included
quickFacts.bestTime: Dry evening, spring to early autumn

arrival.stationExit: Use the main exit and set the first pin before leaving the station entrance.
arrival.pinQuery: Putney Bridge south side London SW15
arrival.firstMove: Walk west from the station to Putney Bridge, cross the Thames towards Putney, then descend to the south-bank riverside at Putney Embankment.

finish.label: The Half Moon Putney, 93 Lower Richmond Road
finish.nearestStation: Putney Bridge Underground or Putney rail
finish.exitNote: Both stations are about a 13–14 minute walk from the pub; choose the one that gives you the simpler journey home.

navigation.disclaimer: Use the live walking route for crossings and temporary closures; the written direction is an orientation cue, not turn-by-turn navigation.

editorial.vibeSummary: Thames air, old rooms and the useful possibility that the evening might become a gig.
bestFor: A southwest escape without Chelsea gloss
editorial.whyNow: Best when there is enough light for a river walk but not enough appetite for a fully programmed night.
editorial.specialBecause: It gives an evening shape without making the shape compulsory.
editorial.whatNotToExpect: More atmospheric than glamorous; the live-music version depends on the current listing.
practicalWarnings: Check opening hours, listings and tickets before setting off.
The river can feel colder and windier than the rest of Putney.
Do not assume a ticket, seat or quiet corner is available.

routeNarrative: Start with the river so the evening has room to arrive. Take one proper pause in a pub, then decide whether the night should remain a conversation or become an event. The no-gig version is the route, not a consolation prize.

stops.name: Putney Bridge — south side
stops.locationNote: Meet on the Putney side of the bridge, above Putney Embankment.
stops.mapQuery: Putney Bridge south side London SW15
stops.directionFromPrevious: From Putney Bridge station, walk to the bridge, cross towards Putney and use the south-bank access for Putney Embankment.
stops.description: Make this the unambiguous meeting point. The bridge gives you river air immediately and avoids deciding where to go while standing outside the station.

stops.name: Putney Embankment and the rowing clubs
stops.directionFromPrevious: Turn west along Putney Embankment and keep the Thames on your right. Extend the walk past the rowing clubs only while the weather and conversation make it worthwhile.
stops.description: Walk by the Thames without treating it as an endurance event. In cold or wind, make this 15–25 minutes; if conversation is easy, let it lengthen.

stops.name: The Half Moon Putney
stops.locationNote: 93 Lower Richmond Road, Putney, London SW15 1EU
stops.mapQuery: The Half Moon Putney 93 Lower Richmond Road SW15 1EU
stops.directionFromPrevious: Leave the riverside for Lower Richmond Road and continue west to number 93. Check the live route rather than improvising through private rowing-club access.
stops.description: Use it as the pub finish even without a gig. Add music only if the current listing, tickets and group mood fit; once the band starts, this stops being a conversation route.

versions.label: River + pub
versions.description: The default: a reversible, low-pressure evening.

versions.label: River + pub + gig
versions.description: Take a shorter walk, eat or drink early, then go only if the listing and tickets work.

copy.dateNotes: Good for a second or third date: walking removes some face-to-face pressure, and either person can leave after the first drink without turning it into a statement.
copy.friendGroupNotes: Best for two to four people. For larger groups, verify seating before making the pub stop the plan.
copy.soloNotes: The no-gig version works alone if you enjoy river walking and sitting somewhere with a book. The gig is a bonus, not a requirement.
copy.foodDrinkNotes: Think snack stop or early practical meal, not a destination dinner. Check kitchen hours before relying on food.
copy.rainyDayBackup: Reduce the walk to one brief river section, begin indoors and keep the gig branch only if weather, transport and tickets still make sense.
copy.continueIfGoingWell: Extend the river walk or take a second verified pub stop. Do not add a third venue just because the route has space.
copy.exitEarly: Leave after the first drink or use Putney station as a practical exit.
copy.finalEditorialNote: River first, pub later, gig if it goes well.

logic.fallbackIfClosed: Keep the river walk and replace the named music stop with any currently open, verified pub room.

links.mapQuery: Putney Bridge to The Half Moon Putney

editorialControl.updateNotes: Venue information requires a field check before public promotion.

title: Bloomsbury After Rain
subtitle: Squares, books and somewhere warm: an indoor-outdoor route for days that should not be written off.

seo.title: Bloomsbury After Rain
seo.description: A rainy-day Bloomsbury route with wet pavements, bookshops, an indoor cultural pause and an old-room finish.

arrival.stationExit: Exit onto Bernard Street and set Russell Square Gardens as the first pin.
arrival.firstMove: Turn left on Bernard Street, then left into Russell Square. Use the nearest open garden entrance rather than circling the whole square.

finish.label: The Lamb, 94 Lamb's Conduit Street
finish.exitNote: The Lamb sits between Russell Square and Holborn; choose the station that makes the journey home simpler.

navigation.disclaimer: Indoor opening hours can change. The map links locate each anchor; check the official venue page before relying on entry, seating or food.

editorial.vibeSummary: Wet stone, old squares and enough indoor pauses to make a rainy day feel intentional.
bestFor: Visitors who have already done the obvious sights
notIdealFor: People who need lots of outdoor space
Anyone looking for a loud evening
editorial.whyNow: It is strongest after rain, when the streets do a little of the work for you.
editorial.specialBecause: The route makes the weather part of the atmosphere rather than a scheduling problem.
editorial.whatNotToExpect: Not a grand museum day or a restaurant crawl; it is deliberately small-scale.
practicalWarnings: Verify the opening hours and entry requirements of any indoor cultural stop.
Bookshops and cafés may not have seating when it is wet.
Keep an umbrella: the route still has short outdoor sections.

routeNarrative: Move between short outdoor sections and warm interiors. The rhythm is the point: square, bookshop, cultural pause, old room. Nothing needs to be booked into an exhausting day.

stops.name: Russell Square Gardens
stops.directionFromPrevious: From Russell Square station, turn left on Bernard Street and left again towards the square; enter through the nearest open gate.
stops.description: Begin outside, even if briefly. Cross the garden or make one small loop; wet paths and old facades earn the first indoor stop.

stops.name: London Review Bookshop
stops.mapQuery: London Review Bookshop 14 Bury Place London WC1A 2JL
stops.directionFromPrevious: Leave the south-west side of Russell Square and walk via Bloomsbury Square towards Bury Place. Let Google Maps handle the final block in heavy rain.
stops.description: Browse without turning the shop into a task. It gives a first date or solo day a useful lull and keeps the outdoor legs short.

stops.name: The British Museum — Great Russell Street entrance
stops.mapQuery: British Museum Great Russell Street entrance WC1B 3DG
stops.directionFromPrevious: Walk south along Bury Place, then turn left onto Great Russell Street for the main entrance.
stops.description: Pick one room, gallery or small exhibition rather than trying to complete the museum. Check current hours and any entry guidance before relying on it.

stops.name: The Lamb, Bloomsbury
stops.mapQuery: The Lamb 94 Lamb's Conduit Street London WC1N 3LZ
stops.directionFromPrevious: Leave via Great Russell Street and head east through Bloomsbury towards Lamb's Conduit Street. Use the live walking route for the quieter final streets.
stops.description: Finish in an old pub room where a silence need not feel awkward. Check current hours and seating before making it a promise.

versions.label: Squares + one indoor stop
versions.description: A 90-minute version for low energy or a short first meeting.

versions.label: Books + culture + warm finish
versions.description: The slower version for an unplanned Sunday.

copy.dateNotes: A good first-date route because the indoor pauses remove pressure and every stop can be the natural ending.
copy.friendGroupNotes: Works for a small group that likes looking at things and talking, not for a noisy birthday plan.
copy.soloNotes: Particularly good alone: browsing, a cultural stop and a warm drink all work without company.
copy.foodDrinkNotes: Food is optional. Plan for a cake, soup or drink rather than relying on a long sit-down meal.
copy.rainyDayBackup: This is already the rainy version. In heavy rain, shorten the first outdoor section and choose two indoor stops close together.
copy.continueIfGoingWell: Add another square or bookshop, then linger over a second drink rather than crossing town for a finale.
copy.exitEarly: Leave after the bookshop or cultural stop via Holborn, Russell Square or Tottenham Court Road.
copy.finalEditorialNote: Better after rain, and happier without a timetable.

logic.fallbackIfClosed: Replace a closed cultural stop with another indoor public space; keep the rhythm of short walk, warm pause, short walk.

links.mapQuery: Russell Square to Holborn via Bloomsbury

editorialControl.updateNotes: Indoor stop and café/pub choice require current verification.

title: Hampstead When You Want to Pretend You Left London
subtitle: Old lanes, heath air and a pub finish with enough weather to make the story better.

seo.title: Hampstead When You Want to Pretend You Left London
seo.description: A Hampstead and heath route for old lanes, hill air, a pub pause and a small escape from usual London.

quickFacts.bestTime: Late afternoon, especially autumn or winter

arrival.stationExit: Outside the station, set Flask Walk as the first pin before choosing a street.
arrival.firstMove: Cross Hampstead High Street into Flask Walk. Follow it east towards Well Walk instead of heading straight north onto the Heath.

finish.label: The Southampton Arms, 139 Highgate Road
finish.exitNote: Gospel Oak is the practical finish. If you skip the pub, walk directly from Parliament Hill Fields to the station.

navigation.disclaimer: Heath paths can be muddy, dark or temporarily diverted. Use the live walking route at each anchor and keep to signed public paths.

editorial.vibeSummary: Lanes, slopes, heath air and the useful sense that London has temporarily loosened its grip.
bestFor: A date that needs walking as well as talking
notIdealFor: People who need a precise, reservation-led day
editorial.whyNow: The route earns its keep in cooler weather, when the streets are quieter and a pub finish feels deserved.
editorial.specialBecause: It feels like a small trip without requiring a real departure.
editorial.whatNotToExpect: Not flat, not cheap-cheap, and not a route for people who want every stop managed in advance.
practicalWarnings: There are hills: do not label this gentle walking.
Weather can change the heath section quickly.
Verify pub opening hours and seating before promising a final stop.

routeNarrative: Let the village streets arrive before the heath. The walk gives a date space to breathe; the pub gives it an ending. The route is less about ticking off Hampstead than about borrowing its sense of distance for an afternoon.

stops.name: Flask Walk into Well Walk
stops.directionFromPrevious: From Hampstead station, cross the High Street into Flask Walk and continue east towards Well Walk.
stops.description: Use Flask Walk and Well Walk as the old-lane introduction. Let the slopes set the pace rather than trying to optimise them away.

stops.name: Keats House garden or museum
stops.mapQuery: Keats House 10 Keats Grove Hampstead NW3 2RR
stops.directionFromPrevious: Continue down Well Walk, then use the signed streets towards Keats Grove. The garden and exterior still work as a pause if the museum is closed.
stops.description: Use the house or garden as the literary pause. Check the official page before relying on museum entry; the route still works when only the exterior is available.

stops.name: Parliament Hill viewpoint
stops.locationNote: South-east corner of Hampstead Heath, above Parliament Hill Fields
stops.mapQuery: Parliament Hill viewpoint Hampstead Heath London
stops.directionFromPrevious: From Keats Grove, enter the Heath on the south side and follow signed public paths towards Parliament Hill. Keep the viewpoint pin open: paths branch and the direct-looking line is not always the public route.
stops.description: Stop for air and distance rather than a perfect photograph. In poor weather, shorten this section and leave via Parliament Hill Fields.

stops.name: The Southampton Arms
stops.mapQuery: The Southampton Arms 139 Highgate Road London NW5 1LE
stops.directionFromPrevious: Leave the viewpoint downhill through Parliament Hill Fields towards Highgate Road, then walk south to number 139. Gospel Oak station remains the simple exit nearby.
stops.description: Finish somewhere old, straightforward and unbookable. The pub says it does not reserve tables, so treat seating as possible rather than guaranteed.

versions.label: Lanes + Keats House
versions.description: Use this if the weather is turning: finish at Hampstead Heath Overground before the Parliament Hill section.

versions.label: Lanes + heath + pub
versions.description: The fuller version for people comfortable with hills and uneven weather.

copy.dateNotes: Better for a first or early date if both people like walking. The short version creates a clean exit before the heath or after the first drink.
copy.friendGroupNotes: Works for a small group that will not resent a hill. Avoid making it the plan for anyone with limited mobility without adapting it first.
copy.soloNotes: A reliable reset route alone, especially if you want a proper change of visual rhythm without leaving London.
copy.foodDrinkNotes: Treat the pub as the food-and-drink decision. Bring a small snack if the walk is the longer version.
copy.rainyDayBackup: Use the short version: Flask Walk, Keats House or its exterior, then Hampstead Heath Overground. Do not force Parliament Hill in difficult weather.
copy.continueIfGoingWell: Continue from Parliament Hill to the Southampton Arms only if the weather and legs are cooperating; otherwise leave via Gospel Oak.
copy.exitEarly: After Keats House, use Hampstead Heath Overground instead of committing to Parliament Hill.
copy.finalEditorialNote: A little uphill, a little gothic, and much better if you do not rush it.

logic.fallbackIfClosed: Make the final stop a café or another verified pub; do not abandon the route because one venue changes hands.

editorialControl.updateNotes: Walking duration, weather route and final venue require a field check.

title: Rotherhithe for River Air and Old Dock Melancholy
subtitle: Warehouses, water and an old-room finish for a quieter stretch of the Thames.

seo.title: Rotherhithe for River Air and Old Dock Melancholy
seo.description: A prototype Rotherhithe route with river walking, dock history and a pub finish.

arrival.stationExit: Meet outside the main station entrance before starting the dock section.
arrival.pinQuery: Stave Hill Ecological Park London SE16
arrival.firstMove: From Canada Water, set Stave Hill as the first pin. Do not improvise towards the river yet; the full riverside sequence still needs a field check.

finish.label: Prototype finish near Bermondsey
finish.exitNote: The finish and riverside access still need field verification.

navigation.disclaimer: Prototype navigation: the starting pin is concrete, but the full route has not been field-checked.

editorial.vibeSummary: Tidal London, old brick and enough quiet for an actual conversation.
notIdealFor: A group that wants constant activity
editorial.whyNow: It works especially well when the river and sky are doing something dramatic.
editorial.specialBecause: The route feels maritime without turning into a heritage lesson.
editorial.whatNotToExpect: More atmospheric than eventful; several stretches are deliberately quiet.
practicalWarnings: Check riverside access and pub hours before going.
Some exposed sections feel colder than the station forecast.
The prototype has not been field-checked.

routeNarrative: Start near the water, follow the older dock edge, pause where the river opens out and finish in a verified pub room. The value is the long middle stretch where nobody has to perform.

stops.name: A practical river start
stops.description: Choose the station that makes arrival easiest, then move towards the river before making any decisions.

stops.name: Dock and warehouse walk
stops.description: Follow a currently accessible riverside section and let the old industrial edges do the atmospheric work.

stops.name: A broad river pause
stops.description: Stop where the Thames feels wide enough to change the pace of the conversation.

stops.name: A verified old-room finish
stops.description: Choose a currently open pub with food or a quiet room; do not make a single venue the whole route.

versions.label: River + one pause
versions.description: A lower-energy version with one riverside stretch and a drink.

versions.label: Dock walk + river + pub
versions.description: The complete version for a slow afternoon.

copy.dateNotes: Better for a second date than a grand first impression.
copy.friendGroupNotes: Best for two to four people who are content to walk and talk.
copy.soloNotes: Works alone if you like water, old brick and a quiet finish.
copy.foodDrinkNotes: Food is optional; verify the final kitchen before relying on it.
copy.rainyDayBackup: Shorten the exposed river stretch and move quickly to the verified indoor stop.
copy.continueIfGoingWell: Continue towards Bermondsey or stay for a second drink.
copy.exitEarly: Leave from Rotherhithe, Canada Water or the nearest practical station before the final stretch.
copy.finalEditorialNote: Grey sky helps. A timetable does not.

logic.fallbackIfClosed: Keep the river sequence and replace the pub with another verified indoor stop.

editorialControl.updateNotes: Test riverside access, timing and final stop.

title: Crystal Palace, Slightly Up a Hill and Away
subtitle: Views, eccentric park details and a pub finish that earns itself.

seo.title: Crystal Palace, Slightly Up a Hill and Away
seo.description: A prototype Crystal Palace route for hills, park oddities and a local pub finish.

arrival.stationExit: Use the park-side station exit and set the dinosaur sculptures as the first pin.
arrival.firstMove: Enter Crystal Palace Park and follow the live walking route towards the dinosaur sculptures. The later hill and pub sequence still needs a field check.

finish.label: Prototype finish around the Crystal Palace Triangle
finish.exitNote: Return to the station after the park if you do not want to test the unverified second half.

navigation.disclaimer: Prototype navigation: the starting pin is concrete, but gradients and the full sequence have not been field-checked.

editorial.vibeSummary: A south London hill with just enough oddness to feel like a small trip.
bestFor: Friends who do not mind a hill
editorial.whyNow: Clear weather gives the route its distance and scale.
editorial.specialBecause: The park is eccentric enough to stop the day feeling managed.
editorial.whatNotToExpect: The hill is real, and the route is more peculiar than picturesque.
practicalWarnings: Do not describe this as gentle walking.
Check park access and the final stop before going.
The prototype has not been field-checked.

routeNarrative: Start with the climb, use the park as the strange middle chapter, then finish around the local high street rather than racing back to central London.

stops.name: Station and first climb
stops.description: Take the hill at a conversational pace and establish early that this is not the flat option.

stops.name: Park oddities
stops.description: Choose a currently open park section with enough eccentric detail to justify the journey.

stops.name: A broad view
stops.description: Pause where the city feels usefully far away.

stops.name: Local pub finish
stops.description: End somewhere currently verified and unfussy rather than making a reservation the climax.

versions.label: Park + view
versions.description: The cheaper daylight version.

versions.label: Park + local finish
versions.description: The fuller weekend version.

copy.dateNotes: Better when both people like walking and a little oddness.
copy.friendGroupNotes: Good for a small group; agree about the hill before arrival.
copy.soloNotes: A useful solo reset if you want distance and visual change.
copy.foodDrinkNotes: Treat food as optional until the final stop is verified.
copy.rainyDayBackup: Use a shorter park circuit and move to an indoor local stop; avoid in severe weather.
copy.continueIfGoingWell: Stay locally for another drink rather than returning to central immediately.
copy.exitEarly: Return to Crystal Palace station after the first park section.
copy.finalEditorialNote: The hill is the price of admission.

logic.fallbackIfClosed: Keep the park and view; choose another verified local room.

links.mapQuery: Crystal Palace station park circular walk

editorialControl.updateNotes: Test park route, gradients and weekend noise.

title: Stoke Newington for Books, Graves and a Decent Pint
subtitle: A gentle north London route with quiet pauses and somewhere warm at the end.

seo.title: Stoke Newington for Books, Graves and a Decent Pint
seo.description: A prototype Stoke Newington route with books, cemetery paths and a pub finish.

arrival.stationExit: Meet outside the station before choosing the first indoor stop.
arrival.pinLabel: Abney Park — Stoke Newington High Street entrance
arrival.pinQuery: Abney Park Stoke Newington High Street entrance London
arrival.firstMove: From the station, set the High Street entrance to Abney Park as the first confirmed outdoor anchor. The bookshop and pub choices still need a field check.

finish.label: Prototype finish near Stoke Newington Church Street
finish.exitNote: Keep the station as the default exit until the final venue is verified.

navigation.disclaimer: Prototype navigation: the outdoor anchor is concrete, but venue choices and opening times have not been field-checked.

editorial.vibeSummary: Books, old trees and a pub room without a great deal of ceremony.
bestFor: A first date with natural pauses
editorial.whyNow: Rain and colder light make the route more convincing, not less.
editorial.specialBecause: It gives a short afternoon several changes of pace without much travel.
editorial.whatNotToExpect: Not a brunch crawl and not a fashionable grand entrance.
practicalWarnings: Check cemetery access and bookshop hours.
Do not promise pub seating.
The prototype has not been field-checked.

routeNarrative: Browse first, walk somewhere quiet, then finish in a verified pub. Each stage can be the natural ending, which is useful for first dates and low-energy days.

stops.name: A currently open bookshop
stops.description: Use browsing as the first low-pressure activity rather than filling every silence.

stops.name: Quiet streets
stops.description: Take the slower residential route rather than the most efficient one.

stops.name: Cemetery or old-tree pause
stops.description: Check access first and skip this section if the company or weather makes it wrong.

stops.name: Verified pub finish
stops.description: Choose a room suited to conversation, with current hours checked.

versions.label: Books + one drink
versions.description: A clean 90-minute first-date version.

versions.label: Books + walk + pub
versions.description: The slower solo or Sunday version.

copy.dateNotes: Every stop creates a natural exit, which is the point.
copy.friendGroupNotes: Keep the group small enough for bookshops and quiet rooms.
copy.soloNotes: One of the stronger solo prototypes because browsing and walking need no audience.
copy.foodDrinkNotes: Plan for a drink or snack, not a guaranteed proper meal.
copy.rainyDayBackup: Shorten the outdoor section and use the bookshop plus verified pub version.
copy.continueIfGoingWell: Add a second quiet room or keep walking locally.
copy.exitEarly: Leave after the bookshop or before the cemetery section.
copy.finalEditorialNote: A place where silence need not be repaired.

logic.fallbackIfClosed: Keep the bookshop-walk-pub rhythm with verified alternatives.

editorialControl.updateNotes: Test access, seating and rainy-day timings.

title: Walthamstow, Market First, Village Later
subtitle: Start with noise and food, then let the afternoon become smaller and more local.

seo.title: Walthamstow, Market First, Village Later
seo.description: A prototype Walthamstow route from market energy to quieter village streets.

arrival.stationExit: Use the town-centre exit and check that the market is trading before starting.
arrival.pinQuery: Walthamstow Market High Street London E17
arrival.firstMove: Walk from the station towards the High Street and set Walthamstow Market as the first pin. Do not rely on the market section without checking the trading day.

finish.label: Prototype finish in Walthamstow Village
finish.exitNote: The quieter village sequence and final venue still need a field check.

navigation.disclaimer: Prototype navigation: the market start is concrete, but trading times and the full route have not been field-checked.

editorial.vibeSummary: A route that begins with a pulse and knows when to quieten down.
notIdealFor: Someone seeking quiet from the first minute
editorial.whyNow: It works when the market is active and there is enough daylight for the quieter second half.
editorial.specialBecause: The change in pace gives the route an actual narrative.
editorial.whatNotToExpect: The first section is busy; this is not a hushed village fantasy.
practicalWarnings: Verify market days and opening times.
Expect crowding at peak times.
The prototype has not been field-checked.

routeNarrative: Use the market as the social ignition, then walk towards quieter streets and finish somewhere local. The route should get calmer as it goes.

stops.name: Market start
stops.description: Browse and eat only after checking the current market day and trading hours.

stops.name: The quieter transition
stops.description: Leave the busiest section and let the route change register.

stops.name: Village-scale streets
stops.description: Take the smaller streets rather than adding another attraction.

stops.name: Local finish
stops.description: Choose a verified pub or taproom that suits the group and current noise tolerance.

versions.label: Market + walk
versions.description: A cheaper daylight version.

versions.label: Market + village + drink
versions.description: The complete social version.

copy.dateNotes: Better for an established date than a quiet first meeting.
copy.friendGroupNotes: The strongest use case; the noisy start gives the group something to react to.
copy.soloNotes: Possible alone, though the market section is the main event.
copy.foodDrinkNotes: Food matters here; verify trading days before relying on it.
copy.rainyDayBackup: Use a shorter market section and move quickly to a verified indoor finish.
copy.continueIfGoingWell: Stay locally for another drink rather than crossing London again.
copy.exitEarly: Return to Walthamstow Central after the market.

logic.fallbackIfClosed: If the market is not operating, do not pretend the route is unchanged; use the village-only version.

editorialControl.updateNotes: Verify market days, crowd level and final stop.

title: Woolwich for the Barrier, Big Sky and Strange Edges
subtitle: An industrial river walk for when a proper urban hike sounds restorative.

seo.title: Woolwich for the Barrier, Big Sky and Strange Edges
seo.description: A prototype Woolwich and Thames Barrier urban hike with industrial river views.

arrival.stationExit: Meet outside the main Elizabeth line entrance with water, daylight and weather checked.
arrival.firstMove: Walk through the Royal Arsenal development towards the Thames and set Royal Arsenal Riverside as the first pin. Do not begin the long barrier section until path access is field-checked.

finish.label: Prototype finish near Charlton
finish.nearestStation: Charlton rail or Woolwich Elizabeth line
finish.exitNote: The second station, path access and safe turn-back points still need field verification.

navigation.disclaimer: Prototype navigation: only the river start is confirmed; do not treat this as a ready-to-follow urban hike.

editorial.vibeSummary: Concrete, tidal water and enough horizon to clear a noisy head.
bestFor: Two people who genuinely like walking
editorial.whyNow: Dry, clear weather makes the scale visible and the exposed sections tolerable.
editorial.specialBecause: It treats the industrial river as the destination, not an apology.
editorial.whatNotToExpect: Not conventionally pretty, not short and not supported by constant amenities.
practicalWarnings: Check path access, weather and daylight.
Carry water and do not rely on frequent toilets or cafés.
The prototype has not been field-checked.

routeNarrative: Begin in Woolwich, commit to the river and let the industrial scale build. The barrier is an anchor, not a photo stop to rush towards.

stops.name: Woolwich start
stops.description: Sort water, weather and the intended exit before joining the river.

stops.name: Industrial river stretch
stops.description: Follow only a currently open and safe public path; the long exposure is the route’s main demand.

stops.name: Barrier and big-sky pause
stops.description: Stop long enough for the scale to register, then choose whether to continue or turn back.

stops.name: Practical finish
stops.description: Use a verified indoor stop near the chosen station; do not assume it will appear exactly when needed.

versions.label: Shorter out-and-back
versions.description: Turn back at the first major river pause.

versions.label: Full urban hike
versions.description: Continue to the planned second station with daylight in hand.

copy.dateNotes: Not a first-date recommendation unless both people asked for a long industrial walk.
copy.friendGroupNotes: Two people is ideal; larger groups need more planning around pace and stops.
copy.soloNotes: The primary use case, with normal daylight and safety judgement.
copy.foodDrinkNotes: Bring water and a snack; food is not the route’s reliable feature.
copy.rainyDayBackup: Do not force this in heavy rain or high wind; choose another route.
copy.continueIfGoingWell: Complete the longer riverside section and finish near the second station.
copy.exitEarly: Use the planned turn-back point or return to Woolwich before the exposed section continues.
copy.finalEditorialNote: Not pretty. Considerably more useful than that.

logic.fallbackIfClosed: If the river path is closed, choose another route rather than improvising around industrial access.

links.mapQuery: Woolwich to Thames Barrier riverside walk

editorialControl.updateNotes: Test access, daylight timing, exits and amenities.

title: Clerkenwell for an Old-City Evening Without West End Fuss
subtitle: Short streets, old rooms and enough food to make the evening feel considered.

seo.title: Clerkenwell for an Old-City Evening
seo.description: A prototype Clerkenwell evening route with old streets, pub rooms and an optional proper meal.

arrival.stationExit: Meet outside the Cowcross Street entrance before the first old-city leg.
arrival.pinQuery: St John's Gate Clerkenwell London EC1M
arrival.firstMove: From Farringdon, set St John's Gate as the first pin and use the live walking route through Clerkenwell. The pub and meal sequence still needs an evening field check.

finish.label: Prototype finish between Clerkenwell and Barbican
finish.exitNote: Keep Farringdon as the default easy exit until the final venue is verified.

navigation.disclaimer: Prototype navigation: the first landmark is concrete, but evening noise, prices and venue sequence have not been field-checked.

editorial.vibeSummary: Old London compressed into a walk short enough to leave room for dinner.
bestFor: Visitors who want central London without West End chaos
editorial.whyNow: It works after work, especially in cold or wet weather when short outdoor sections are an advantage.
editorial.specialBecause: It feels central without asking anyone to endure the West End.
editorial.whatNotToExpect: Not cheap-cheap and not a sprawling adventure; the value is compression.
practicalWarnings: Check kitchen hours and whether a table is realistic.
Weekday noise changes sharply by venue and time.
The prototype has not been field-checked.

routeNarrative: Begin with one short old-street circuit, take a drink in a verified room, then choose between a proper meal and an easy exit. The route spends money on comfort rather than spectacle.

stops.name: Farringdon start
stops.description: Meet somewhere obvious and keep the first leg short enough for late arrivals.

stops.name: Old-city street circuit
stops.description: Use churches, squares and older lanes as the first chapter, not a heritage checklist.

stops.name: Verified pub room
stops.description: Choose for conversation and current noise, not historical claims alone.

stops.name: Optional proper meal
stops.description: Book only if the group wants commitment; otherwise end after the first drink.

versions.label: Streets + one drink
versions.description: The low-commitment after-work version.

versions.label: Streets + drink + meal
versions.description: The considered evening version.

copy.dateNotes: Strong for an early established date: easy exit first, proper meal if the mood holds.
copy.friendGroupNotes: Good for two to four; larger groups should book rather than improvise.
copy.soloNotes: Possible, but the route earns more with conversation.
copy.foodDrinkNotes: This is the one prototype where a proper meal can be central; verify price and booking.
copy.rainyDayBackup: Shorten the street circuit and move between two close indoor stops.
copy.continueIfGoingWell: Take the proper meal version or continue towards Barbican.
copy.exitEarly: End after the first drink and return to Farringdon.
copy.finalEditorialNote: Central London, with the volume turned down.

logic.fallbackIfClosed: Replace the named type with another verified old-room stop; preserve the short walk and easy exit.

editorialControl.updateNotes: Test weekday noise, prices, booking pressure and exits.

title: King’s Cross for Water, Graves and a Properly Low-Stakes Pint
subtitle: A canal-side start, a quiet churchyard and an easy pub finish for two people who do not need a big plan.

seo.title: King’s Cross for Water, Graves and a Properly Low-Stakes Pint
seo.description: A prototype King’s Cross route for two: Granary Square, Gasholder Park, Camley Street, St Pancras Old Church and a low-pressure pub finish.

arrival.stationExit: Use the King’s Cross exit towards Granary Square rather than meeting in the station concourse.
arrival.firstMove: From King’s Cross, cross the canal-side development towards Granary Square. Make the square the meeting point so neither person has to navigate the station crush.

finish.label: The Queen’s Head, 66 Acton Street
finish.exitNote: The pub is about two minutes from King’s Cross, so either person can leave without making the end of the route feel like a statement.

navigation.disclaimer: Camley Street Natural Park has controlled access. Use the Gasholder Park and churchyard version if it is closed, and follow live maps for crossings.

editorial.vibeSummary: A new-London start that gets greener, older and less performative as it goes.
notIdealFor: Anyone wanting a completely hidden London
editorial.whyNow: It lets King’s Cross be useful without making the route about the station or its restaurants.
editorial.specialBecause: The move from canal infrastructure to churchyard gives a short walk an actual change of mood.
editorial.whatNotToExpect: Not secret, not untouched and not worth forcing after the nature reserve closes.
practicalWarnings: Check Camley Street Natural Park access before relying on it.
The route has not been field-tested yet.
Check current pub hours and noise before going.

routeNarrative: Begin in the part of King’s Cross that looks newly made, then let the route become smaller and stranger. The water and gasholder are the first conversation; the churchyard is the quiet pause; the pub is nearby enough that the plan never feels trapped.

stops.name: Granary Square
stops.locationNote: Granary Square, King’s Cross, London N1C
stops.directionFromPrevious: From King’s Cross St Pancras, follow signs for Granary Square and meet beside the canal rather than inside the station.
stops.description: Use the square only as a clean starting point. Have one lap, decide whether the day needs a coffee, then leave before the development becomes the whole route.

stops.name: Gasholder Park
stops.locationNote: Gasholder Park, King’s Cross, London N1C
stops.mapQuery: Gasholder Park Kings Cross London N1C
stops.directionFromPrevious: Follow the canal-side park east from Granary Square towards the circular gasholder structure.
stops.description: A short, strange industrial pause that gives you something to look at without needing an attraction to explain itself.

stops.name: Camley Street Natural Park — daytime branch
stops.mapQuery: Camley Street Natural Park London N1C 4PW
stops.directionFromPrevious: From Gasholder Park, follow the live walking route to Camley Street. Enter only if the reserve is open; otherwise skip directly to St Pancras Old Churchyard.
stops.description: The green interruption is the point. If it is closed, do not replace it with another paid stop: keep the route short and move on.

stops.name: St Pancras Old Churchyard
stops.locationNote: St Pancras Old Church, Pancras Road, London NW1 2BA
stops.mapQuery: St Pancras Old Church London NW1 2BA
stops.directionFromPrevious: Walk south from Camley Street through the quieter streets towards Pancras Road. If the park was closed, use this as the first proper pause instead.
stops.description: Take a slow lap of the churchyard rather than treating it as a history lesson. It is the part that makes the route feel less like a redeveloped district.

stops.name: The Queen’s Head
stops.mapQuery: The Queen's Head 66 Acton Street London WC1X 9NB
stops.directionFromPrevious: From the churchyard, head south-east to Acton Street. Keep King’s Cross behind you until you are ready to leave; the station is still only a short walk away.
stops.description: A deliberately close finish: there is room for a pint and a conversation, but no obligation to turn it into dinner or a second venue.

versions.label: Canal + nature + pub
versions.description: The full version when Camley Street Natural Park is open.

versions.label: Gasholder + churchyard + pub
versions.description: Skip the reserve after access hours and keep the route short, calm and useful.

copy.dateNotes: Good for a second or third date because the first hour is visibly structured but every point creates a natural exit.
copy.friendGroupNotes: Works for two to four people if everyone is content with a short walk rather than a packed itinerary.
copy.soloNotes: The daytime version works alone, though the close-by pub finish is less essential.
copy.foodDrinkNotes: Treat the pub as drinks first. Check current food and seating before making it dinner.
copy.rainyDayBackup: Skip the nature reserve, keep Gasholder Park brief and go straight to the churchyard or finish early in the pub.
copy.continueIfGoingWell: Stay for another drink, or return to Granary Square for a final canal-side lap rather than adding a third venue.
copy.exitEarly: Leave from King’s Cross after Gasholder Park, or from the churchyard before the pub finish.
copy.finalEditorialNote: A station district that gets less station-like the longer you let it breathe.

logic.fallbackIfClosed: If Camley Street Natural Park is closed, do the after-hours version; do not substitute a random indoor attraction.

links.mapQuery: Granary Square to The Queen's Head Acton Street via Gasholder Park and St Pancras Old Church

editorialControl.updateNotes: Test the Camley Street access branch, timings, crossings and final pub noise before promotion.
